name: Marty Ehrlich
abstract: Marty Ehrlich (born May 31, 1955) is a multi-instrumentalist (saxophones, clarinets, flutes) and is considered one of the leading proponents of the 1980's New York City "downtown" avant-garde jazz era.

concerts.label: Marty Ehrlich's Emergency Peace Ensemble/ Don Byron's Semaphore

works.t: the tucked sleeve of a one-armed boy

works_created.t: the tucked sleeve of a one-armed boy

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Now I remember when Marty Ehrlich was with me… 'Tenor Madness'? ... This one had Marty Ehrlich, John Purcell, Howard Johnson, Rufus Reid and yourself
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Peplowski
about_quotes.evidence: I’m talking to this clarinet player Marty Ehrlich about doing some recording together
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Peplowski interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Vincent Pelote
about_quotes.evidence: I just heard some Marty Ehrlich that I thought was fantastic.
about_quotes.doc_title: Vincent Pelote interviewed by Monk Rowe, Orlando, Florida, February 25, 2017
